backend_selection_owner: installed Codex backend selected by the authenticated host account and workspace; Switchloom only declares repository-local project agent model and effort requests

trust_and_discovery.trust_boundary: trusted project repository plus generated .codex/config.toml [agents.*] registrations; global Codex configuration is outside Switchloom ownership
trust_and_discovery.discovery_behavior: Codex discovers repository-local agent files through .codex/config.toml registration entries after project trust and host reload/restart

parallelism.source: Codex V2 collaboration host exposes four active agent slots including the root, leaving three parallel child slots in the current runtime

role_precedence: spawn_agent agent_type selects the registered project agent file
the selected project agent file declares requested child model and model_reasoning_effort fields when present
optional custom-agent model and model_reasoning_effort inherit from the parent session when omitted
parent live sandbox and approval choices are reapplied when spawning a child
the live Codex backend remains the authority for effective model availability, billing, timing, and orchestration

backend_selection_owner.source: OpenAI Codex configuration reference: project-scoped config cannot override machine-local provider, auth, host-owned app request metadata, configuration profile selection, or telemetry routing keys.
backend_selection_owner.observed_value: installed Codex backend selected by the authenticated host account and workspace; Switchloom only declares repository-local project agent model and effort requests
required_raw_fragments: Project-scoped config cannot override machine-local provider, auth
backend_selection_owner.raw_output: Project-scoped config cannot override machine-local provider, auth, host-owned app request metadata, notification, configuration profile selection, or telemetry routing keys; provider/auth/profile routing belongs in user-level config or host state.

trust_and_discovery.source: OpenAI Codex configuration and subagents references: trusted project-scoped config plus project-scoped .codex/agents custom agent files.

observed_value.trust_boundary: trusted project repository plus generated .codex/config.toml [agents.*] registrations; global Codex configuration is outside Switchloom ownership
observed_value.discovery_behavior: Codex discovers repository-local agent files through .codex/config.toml registration entries after project trust and host reload/restart

required_raw_fragments: project-scoped config files only when you trust the project
trust_and_discovery.raw_output: Codex loads project-scoped config files only when you trust the project. To define project-scoped custom agents, add standalone TOML files under .codex/agents/.

parallelism.source: Codex session developer runtime contract for this workspace declares the active collaboration slot budget.

observed_value.source: Codex V2 collaboration host exposes four active agent slots including the root, leaving three parallel child slots in the current runtime

parallelism.raw_output: Current Codex session runtime contract: There are 4 available concurrency slots, meaning that up to 4 agents can be active at once, including the root thread; therefore a root session can run at most 3 parallel child agents.

role_precedence.source: OpenAI Codex subagents reference: parent live sandbox and approval overrides are reapplied to children; omitted custom-agent model and model_reasoning_effort inherit from the parent session.
observed_value: spawn_agent agent_type selects the registered project agent file
the selected project agent file declares requested child model and model_reasoning_effort fields when present
optional custom-agent model and model_reasoning_effort inherit from the parent session when omitted
parent live sandbox and approval choices are reapplied when spawning a child
the live Codex backend remains the authority for effective model availability, billing, timing, and orchestration
required_raw_fragments: reapplies the parent turn live runtime overrides
model_reasoning_effort inherit from the parent session when omitted
role_precedence.raw_output: Codex also reapplies the parent turn live runtime overrides when it spawns a child, including sandbox and approval choices, even if the selected custom agent file sets different defaults. Optional custom-agent fields such as model and model_reasoning_effort inherit from the parent session when omitted.

shared_filesystem.source: Codex session developer runtime contract for this workspace declares shared filesystem behavior for team agents.
required_raw_fragments: All agents share the same container and filesystem
edits made by one agent are immediately visible to all other agents
shared_filesystem.raw_output: Current Codex team runtime contract: All agents share the same container and filesystem, use the same current working directory, and edits made by one agent are immediately visible to all other agents.

delegation_modes.source: OpenAI Codex subagents and models references: Ultra can proactively delegate; most intelligence levels require explicit delegation.
required_raw_fragments: With Ultra, ChatGPT can proactively delegate work
At most intelligence levels, ask for delegation explicitly
delegation_modes.raw_output: With Ultra, ChatGPT can proactively delegate work when parallel agents would materially improve speed or quality. At most intelligence levels, ask for delegation explicitly.

negative_contracts: do not certify Codex V2 with a different installed version unless the capability evidence id and host_version_constraints are updated
do not accept source references without the expected digest-bound evidence document
do not accept child slot counts other than the frozen three-child V2 contract
do not enable automatic delegation outside the explicit Ultra mode boundary
do not accept a contract whose runtime facts differ from this parsed evidence even when the source reference digest matches these bytes
